The Problem
Good Business.
Invisible Online.

JT Junk Solutions was already doing solid work on the ground — crews, trucks, and a reputation for reliability. But online, they were invisible. No consistent content, no YouTube presence, and zero infrastructure to turn attention into booked jobs. They were leaving thousands on the table every single month.

"Most agencies told him to get more views. We told him views without a conversion system are just entertainment."

— Bakes Media
Our Approach
Revenue Infrastructure.
Not Just Content.

We didn't just make videos. We built a full short-to-long content funnel — short-form clips on Reels and Shorts to grab attention, pushing viewers to long-form YouTube breakdowns designed to build trust and book jobs. Every piece of content had one job: move someone closer to picking up the phone.

"We treat YouTube like a sales rep that works 24/7 and never asks for commission."

— Bakes Media
$50,000 Revenue in Month 1
20K Subscribers Gained
5.7M Views Accumulated

How We Did It
The 3-Part System

Every result we've ever gotten traces back to the same three-part execution. No tricks, no hacks — just the right content, the right conversion layer, and the right consistency.

1
Content That Converts

We scripted and produced long-form YouTube videos specifically designed to answer buyer questions, showcase job quality, and build credibility — not just rack up views. Every video was a silent sales pitch.

2
Short-Form as a Traffic Engine

Reels and Shorts were cut from the long-form content and optimized to stop the scroll. Their job wasn't to entertain — it was to push people to the full video and into the booking funnel.

3
Conversion Infrastructure

We set up clear CTAs in every video, optimized the channel for booking intent, and built a system that turned viewers into quote requests. The content didn't just build an audience — it booked jobs.

The Framework
Short to Long Funnel

This is the exact funnel architecture we deployed for JT Junk Solutions. From a stranger seeing a 30-second Reel to a booked job — every stage is engineered, not accidental.

The Proof
Before & After — The Numbers

These aren't projections. This is real dashboard data from JT Junk Solutions' YouTube analytics and their booking system — showing exactly what changed after we stepped in.

YouTube Analytics — Before vs After

Subscribers jumping +12.3K and direct revenue tied to the channel climbing measurably after our system went live.

Revenue Dashboard — Attribution

Gross revenue at $18,702 with 76 new users tracked — this is what happens when content has a conversion system behind it, not just a subscribe button.

What This Looks Like In Practice
Month-by-Month Execution
Week 1–2

Discovery & Content Audit

We audited the existing channel, identified highest-performing content themes in the junk removal niche, and built out a 90-day content roadmap aligned to booking intent keywords.

Week 3–4

Production & Funnel Setup

Scripted and produced the first batch of long-form videos. Set up channel CTAs, booking links, and short-form clips designed to drive traffic back to YouTube. Conversion infrastructure was live before the first video dropped.

Month 1

$50,000 in Tracked Revenue

With the funnel running, content publishing on schedule, and short-form distribution pushing viewers into the channel — the phone started ringing. $50K in job revenue traced directly back to content in month one.

Ongoing

Compounding Results

YouTube is a long-game asset. Every video continues generating views, clicks, and bookings long after it's published. JT Junk Solutions now has a content library that works 24/7 as a sales engine.
